articles.title: Homemade bomb thrown at protest near New York City mayor’s house, police say
articles.description:
The counterprotester accused of throwing the bomb was one of six people arrested after a clash with anti-Muslim protesters led by the right-wing activist Jake Lang.

The post Homemade bomb thrown at protest near New York City mayor’s house, police say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Homemade bomb thrown during protest near NYC mayor's residence on March 8.
Six individuals, including counterprotester, arrested after clash with anti-Muslim protesters.
Increased tensions may affect local Boston protests and community safety measures.

articles.title: Iran names Mojtaba Khamenei to succeed his slain father as supreme leader
articles.description:
The younger Khamenei, who had not been seen or heard from publicly since the war started, had long been considered a contender for the post, even before an Israeli strike killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

The post Iran names Mojtaba Khamenei to succeed his slain father as supreme leader appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Iran names Mojtaba Khamenei as new supreme leader following his father's death on March 8, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei had been a contender for leadership even before the Israeli strike.
Boston residents may see increased tensions in Middle Eastern relations affecting local communities.

articles.title: NBA coach dubs Derrick White as a ‘top-5 player’ in NBA
articles.description:
"You look at all the advanced stuff, he’s [a] top 5 player in the league, [a] superstar."

The post NBA coach dubs Derrick White as a ‘top-5 player’ in NBA appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: NBA coach Kenny Atkinson labels Derrick White a top-5 player in the league.
White's performance boosts Boston Celtics' chances for success in the upcoming season.
Local fans celebrate White's recognition, enhancing community pride in the Celtics.

articles.title: Crude oil prices surpass $100 a barrel as the Iran war impedes production and shipping
articles.description:
The price for a barrel of Brent crude, the international standard, was at $101.19 shortly after trading resumed on the Chicago Mercantile Exchange, up 9.2% from its settlement price of $92.69 Friday.

The post Crude oil prices surpass $100 a barrel as the Iran war impedes production and shipping appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Brent crude oil prices reached $101.19 per barrel, up 9.2% from Friday's price.
The rise in oil prices is attributed to the ongoing Iran war affecting production.
Boston residents may face higher gas prices and increased costs for goods and services.
